title: Tapping for I Can't Afford to Decorate
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for when the home feels unloved but the budget is tight
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for when the home needs attention and the money for it isn't there. The script addresses the specific disappointment of loving home and not being able to do what you can picture.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you can see what your home needs and the budget to do it isn't available, and the gap between the two feels heavy.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the gap between what your home could be and what the budget allows right now. Use it when the restriction is real and the disappointment in it is real, and you need somewhere to put both.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I can see exactly what this home needs and I cannot afford any of it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though it hurts to care about my home this much and not be able to do anything about it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the gap between what I can picture and what I can afford feels too wide, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** I can see what this home needs.
**Side of eye:** The budget isn't there.
**Under eye:** I am tired of looking at what isn't done yet.
**Under nose:** I want to love this home.
**Chin:** The restriction is real.
**Collarbone:** The disappointment in it is real.
**Under arm:** I wish it were different.
**Top of head:** I'm putting the picture down for today.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** My home is more than the list of things it still needs.
**Side of eye:** I am allowed to look at what is already here.
**Under eye:** Small is still something.
**Under nose:** I can love this home as it is and want more for it.
**Chin:** The restriction is temporary.
**Collarbone:** I know what I'm building toward.
**Under arm:** I can find one thing to shift today without spending.
**Top of head:** This home is enough to live in today.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy.
